name: cyberware governance pipeline
description: The infra's OWN blueprint — the path every task-ledger takes through the governed runtime: validate -> compose -> compile -> oversee -> execute. The same L++ formalism the skills use, turned on the framework itself (the ouroboros). Each gate's failure route is the stage refusing and logging.

reviewed_against.verdict: model CURRENT — the lifecycle shape (states/gates/transitions) is unchanged. The changes since the prior review (v1.6.0 enforcement hardening: per-snippet integrity via snippetverify, grantverify hardening, fail-closed vault/rails; the cargo + params ACL axes) are WITHIN-GATE enforcement inside g_governed, not new stages. Confirmed by module-mapping (validator/composer/compiler/oversight/executor present, unchanged shape).
reviewed_against.h0_rule: self-blueprint must be no older than the last enforcement-surface commit (ci-codeqc/gate)

submitted.description: a task-ledger (skill, perk, vars, record_store) is submitted; nothing has run

validated.description: validator.py confirmed the claims are real — store writable, runtime + binaries reachable, required inputs present

composed.description: composer.py model-checked the L++ blueprint (-> TLC) — no logical deadlock

compiled.description: compiler.py turned ledger + manifesto + contracts + snippets into ONE step-wise bash script

cleared.description: oversight.py scanned the script against OVERSIGHT_RULE — no unapproved destructive/dangerous patterns

executed.description: executor.py ran it through the governed channel — tamper-checked, upstream-gated, recorded to the run-ledger

g_oversight_clear.description: the script clears the oversight rules

g_governed.description: no drift (tamper-check incl. per-snippet integrity via snippetverify); oversight re-checked in-channel; on a delegated step the per-actor ACL (skill/tier/secret/params/cargo axes) is re-enforced off-node by exod; predecessors recorded

a_validate.description: probe the runtime, dirs, and required inputs

a_compose.description: compile L++ -> TLA+ -> TLC (structural fallback if no JRE)

a_compile.description: ledger + manifesto + contracts + snippets -> step-wise bash

a_oversee.description: regex/ast scan the compiled script against OVERSIGHT_RULE

a_execute.description: the ONLY channel: tamper-check, in-channel OVERSIGHT_RULE scan, upstream gate, provenance record per step

safety_invariants.name: governed_execution_only
safety_invariants.description: GUARDRAIL: a task reaches 'executed' ONLY through executor.py — the single governed channel.

safety_invariants.name: no_stage_skipped
safety_invariants.expression: ordered(submitted, validated, composed, compiled, cleared, executed)
safety_invariants.description: GUARDRAIL: no stage is skipped — each gate must hold before the next fires.

safety_invariants.name: refuse_on_drift
safety_invariants.description: GUARDRAIL: if the compiled script drifted from its .bk snapshot, the executor refuses.

safety_invariants.name: record_every_run
safety_invariants.description: GUARDRAIL: every governed run is appended to the run-ledger — no out-of-band execution.
